prompt_md:
Your team ships an AI support assistant that retrieves customer tickets from a **vector store** and feeds them to a model. You already redact every prompt an agent types. A privacy review finds that the **vector store was built from the full, un-redacted ticket history**, and that you **log the assembled prompt** (typed text plus retrieved ticket content) to a shared observability platform.

You can make **one** change next. Given that the sensitive PII enters through ingestion and retrieved context — not the typed prompt — which change best addresses the root cause, and why?

decision_choices: Tighten the redaction filter that runs on the agent's typed prompt only
Classify and redact PII at ingestion (before embedding), isolate the store per tenant, and exclude the assembled prompt from plain logs
Switch to a model with a larger context window so it retrieves fewer tickets
Lower the model's temperature so its answers are more deterministic
reason_choices: Because the leak surfaces are upstream — the vector store and the logs hold raw PII regardless of the typed-prompt redaction, so controlling ingestion, isolation, and logging addresses the root cause
Because redacting the typed prompt also redacts the retrieved context and the vector store
Because a larger context window prevents customer data from being embedded
Because deterministic answers cannot contain personal data
